title.value: SurveyX: Academic Survey Automation via Large Language Models
title.evidence: SurveyX: Academic Survey Automation via Large Language Models

value: SurveyX significantly enhances the efficacy of automated survey composition by decomposing the process into two phases: Preparation and Generation.
The system introduces online reference retrieval and a preprocessing method called AttributeTree, improving the quality of references used in surveys.
Experimental results show that SurveyX outperforms existing systems in content quality (0.259 improvement) and citation quality (1.76 enhancement), closely approaching human expert performance.
insights.evidence: Experimental evaluation results show that SurveyX outperforms existing automated survey generation systems in content quality (0.259 improvement) and citation quality (1.76 enhancement), approaching human expert performance across multiple evaluation dimensions.

research_background.value: The exponential growth of literature, with arXiv.org receiving about a thousand new papers daily, has made it increasingly challenging for researchers to comprehend technological evolution in specific subfields.
research_background.evidence: Statistics indicate that arXiv.org receives about a thousand new papers daily. The exponential growth of literature has made it increasingly challenging for researchers attempting to comprehend the technological evolution and developmental trajectories of specific subfields from the ground up.

main_contributions.value: SurveyX proposes an efficient reference retrieval algorithm, a reference preprocessing method called AttributeTree, an outline generation method named Outline Optimization, and an evaluation framework that incorporates additional metrics.
main_contributions.evidence: To sum up, our main contributions are as follows: We propose an efficient reference retrieval algorithm capable of expanding keywords based on a given topic... We design a reference pre-processing method called AttributeTree... We introduce an outline generation method named Outline Optimization...

core_approach.value: SurveyX divides the survey composing process into two phases: Preparation Phase and Generation Phase, utilizing retrieval algorithms, the AttributeTree method, and a structured outline generation approach.
core_approach.evidence: SurveyX divides the composing process of surveys into two phases: the Preparation Phase and the Generation Phase. In the Preparation Phase, SurveyX employs retrieval algorithms... In the Generation Phase, SurveyX utilizes the information obtained in the previous phase to sequentially generate the outline and main body of the survey.

reference_acquisition.value: SurveyX employs a module for acquiring references that includes both offline and online data sources, utilizing a self-developed crawler system based on Google Scholar.
reference_acquisition.evidence: Considering the current shortage of effective tools for efficiently acquiring a large volume of highly relevant references, we independently develop a module for acquiring references... The online data source is a self-developed crawler system based on Google Scholar.

retrieval_algorithms.value: The retrieval algorithm consists of reference recall and reference filter steps, utilizing a Keyword Expansion Algorithm for comprehensive reference collection.
retrieval_algorithms.evidence: The retrieval algorithm is divided into two steps: reference recall and reference filter... The goal of the reference recall step is to avoid missing any references related to the topic as much as possible.

reference_preprocessing.value: The AttributeTree method categorizes references and extracts key information to enhance information density and context window utilization.
reference_preprocessing.evidence: We design a reference pre-processing method called AttributeTree... This method significantly increases the information density of the reference materials and efficiently utilizes the context window of LLMs.

outline_generation.value: Outline Optimization generates outlines based on hints derived from the AttributeTree, employing a two-step process to enhance logical rigor.
outline_generation.evidence: We designed an outline generation method named Outline Optimization... This method consists of two steps.

content_generation.value: The content generation stage utilizes hints from the secondary outline to guide the generation of the main body content, ensuring consistency across sections.
content_generation.evidence: During the content generation stage, inspired by the human writing process for surveys, we continue to use the hint-based method employed during the outline generation stage to enhance the depth of the generated content.

post_optimization.value: The post refinement stage enhances content quality and presentation by incorporating a RAG-based rewriting module and a figure and table generation module.
post_optimization.evidence: Based on this, we design a post refinement stage, which polishes the initial draft with two main goals: (1) to improve the quality of the content... (2) to add figures and tables to enrich the presentation of the survey.
